summary: Ranked 21st by FIFA but seeking a first-ever knockout berth (group exits in all six appearances since 1978). Under Amir Ghalenoei they sit deep and spring forward on the break — pragmatic and direct, with 'no expectations to win style points' (Ghalenoei called expansive football 'time-consuming').
style: Sit deep and counter (without Egypt's man-to-man), attacking vertically and direct. Veterans keep it organised out of possession. Can play incisive short passes when afforded space — Taremi drifts wide and dovetails with the runs of winger Mohammad Mohebbi.

counterReliance.note: Deep block, vertical and direct on the counter

strengths: Organised, experienced spine (Ghoddos, Ezatolahi; veterans Khalilzadeh, Hajsafi)
Taremi's hold-up, link play and finishing to lead the break
Mohebbi's runs in behind, dovetailing with Taremi
weaknesses: Lack genuine creative threats in midfield
Left out Sardar Azmoun (57 in 91) — thins the attacking options behind Taremi
Defensive spaces if the deep block is stretched

keyPlayers.name: Mehdi Taremi
keyPlayers.note: Iran's second-highest scorer (behind Ali Daei's 110); 2 goals + an assist at the 2022 WC and 8 in 13 qualifiers — the focal point of everything

keyPlayers.name: Mohammad Mohebbi
keyPlayers.note: Dynamic runner in behind whose movement could be vital alongside Taremi

tactics: Defend deep, stay compact and organised, and break vertically through Taremi and Mohebbi — with the odd incisive short-passing move when given room. Won't win plaudits for style, but tough to break down.
notes: Six World Cup appearances since 1978, all ending in the group stage.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group G (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
